title: Mausalaparvan - Canto 001
translation_text:
Vaishampayana said: Thus addressed by his maternal uncle, the scorcher of enemies, Bibhatsu, was sad and dejected in mind. He spoke to Vasudeva.

O lord, I am not able to live long without the best of the Vrishnis and my maternal uncle Madhu.

King, Bhimasena, Saha-deva, Pandava,  Nakula, and Yajnaseni, we six are of one mind.

And this is certainly the time for the king's departure. Know this to be the time that has arrived, O best of those who know time.

In every way, I will take the women of the Vrishnis, the children and the elderly,  and bring them to Indraprastha, O destroyer of enemies.

Having said this, Dhananjaya spoke these words to Dāruka: "I wish to see the ministers of the Vrishni heroes soon."

Having spoken thus to the assembly of Yadavas, Arjuna, the hero, entered, grieving for the great warriors.

Then all the Prajapatis and the Brahmanas and the citizens, having approached, stood around him who was seated on the seat.

To all those who were dejected, silent, and lost their minds, Partha himself, even more dejected, spoke these words then:

I myself will lead the Vrishni and Andhaka people to Shakra-prastha. But this entire city will be flooded by the ocean.

Make ready the chariots and various jewels. This Vajra will be your king in Śakraprastha.

And on the seventh day, when the sun has risen clear, we shall all go outside. Be ready without delay.

Thus addressed by him, the citizens, by the one of unwearying deeds, and being eager for their own success, quickly made ready.

Partha spent that night in Keshava's house,  suddenly overwhelmed by great grief and delusion.

Then, the next day, the powerful Vasudeva, the son of Shura, united with the great splendor, went to the supreme destination.

Then there was a great sound in Vasudeva's house, of women crying and wailing.

All of them had dishevelled hair and were without ornaments and garlands. With their hands they beat their breasts and lamented piteously.

Devaki, Bhadra, Rohini, and Madri, the best of women, were determined to follow their husband.

Then, O descendant of Bharata, Partha had the horse-drawn chariot with many garlands brought out to take Shouri out.

The citizens of Dvārakā, O bull among men, were all struck by grief and sorrow and followed him here and there.

The sacrificial horse, the umbrella, and the blazing fires went ahead of his chariot, followed by the priests.

And the goddesses, adorned, followed that hero, Surrounded by thousands of women and thousands of brides.

The place which was dear to the great soul while he was alive, there they performed the funeral rites for him.

And the women of the harem, the wives of the hero, followed the hero who had entered the funeral pyre. Then the four wives followed him, going to the world of their husband.

The son of Pandu, together with the four women, burned him with sandalwood and various kinds of incense.

Then there arose the sound of the burning fire, and the sound of the chanting of the Sama-s, and even the weeping of men.

Then those young heroes of the Vrishni clan, headed by Vajra, all performed the water ritual for the great-souled women.

Having performed that rite, Phalguna, who had not lost his dharma, went to where the Vrishnis had perished, O bull of the Bharatas.

Seeing them fallen in the battle, he was extremely distressed. The son of Kuru became very distressed and did what was appropriate.

And he performed all the rites according to the principal. Those who were killed by the curse of Brahma, by the pestles born from the plow.

Then, having searched for the bodies of Rama and Vasudeva, he had them cremated by men who had accomplished their tasks.

Having performed the funeral rites for them in accordance with the prescribed rites, the son of Pandu quickly mounted his chariot on the seventh day and departed. With chariots yoked to horses, and also with donkeys and camels.

The women of the Vrishni heroes, tormented by grief, wept and followed the great-souled son of Pandu, Dhananjaya.

The servants of the Andhakas and Vrishnis, the charioteers and the horsemen, and the aged and the children, who were without heroes, and the citizens and the countrymen, went, following the orders of Partha, surrounding the women.

With elephants and elephant riders, and with those like mountains, They went together with foot soldiers and with those having weapons.

All the sons of the Andhakas and Vrishnis were devoted to Partha. Brahmanas, Kshatriyas, Vaishyas, and Shudras of great wealth,

The ten thousand six hundred were the protectors of Vasudeva. Having placed in front the grandson of the wise Krishna, they went to Vajra.

And many thousands, millions, and billions of women of the Bhojas, Vrishnis, and Andhakas, without their lords, went out.

The wheel of the Vrishnis, which was like the ocean, which was endowed with great power, was borne by Partha, the best of charioteers, the conqueror of enemy cities.

When the people had left, the ocean, the abode of sharks, flooded Dwarkā, filled with jewels, with water.

Seeing that wonder, the people of Dvārakā quickly went, saying, "Oh, it is fate."

In the delightful forests, on the mountains and on the rivers, Dhananjaya, living there, did not bring the Vrishni women.

Having reached the land of Pañcanada, the wise one, who was very prosperous, made his abode in a place rich in cows, cattle, and grain.

Then the greed arose in the bandits, whose lords had been killed, seeing the women being led away by Partha alone, O descendant of Bharata.

Then those wicked ones, their minds overcome by greed, the robbers, having met, spoke together, seeing evil.

This one Arjuna is the warrior who has killed the old and the young and the lord of horses. He is leading us beyond the reach of the warriors who have lost their energy.

Then the robbers, armed with sticks, in thousands, rushed against the Vrishnis, the thieves who had stolen the cattle.

With a great lion's roar, they were driving away the ordinary people, They rushed forward for wealth, urged by the passage of time.

Then Kounteya, with his followers, suddenly turned back. Arjuna, the mighty-armed one, smilingly said to them:

If you do not wish to die, turn back, you who know not Dharma. Do not lament now, being pierced by arrows, that you are slain by me.

Thus addressed by the hero, disregarding his words, the foolish ones rushed towards the people, being repeatedly restrained.

Then Arjuna began to string the divine, ageless, great bow Gandiva, as if with difficulty.

In the midst of the confusion, he strung his bow with difficulty. And he thought of the weapons, but could not remember them either.

Seeing that great feat of his in the battle, and the destruction of the great divine weapons, he was ashamed.

All the warriors of the Vrishnis, mounted on elephants, horses, chariots and foot-soldiers, were unable to turn back the people being carried away.

But because of the large number of wives, the people were running here and there. So Partha made an effort to protect the people.

While all the warriors were watching, those best of women were dragged away in all directions, and others went forth out of desire.

Then, with arrows released from Gandiva, Partha Dhananjaya, together with the servants of the Vrishnis, killed the demons in great agitation.

In a moment, O king, those arrows of his, which were straight, became exhausted.  For, having become inexhaustible before, they became exhausted, feeding on blood.

Having reached the exhaustion of arrows, he was overcome with sorrow and grief.  Then the son of Paka killed the demons with a bundle of bows.

While Partha looked on, the best of the Vrishni and Andhaka women  were carried off by the Mlecchas, O Janamejaya.

Dhananjaya, the lord, thought about that destiny in his mind. Overwhelmed with sorrow and grief, he became absorbed in sighing.

And by the destruction of weapons, and by the exhaustion of the strength of arms, And by the unruliness of the bow, and by the exhaustion of arrows,

Partha was distressed, thinking that it was fate. Then, O king, he turned back and said, "This is not there."

Then, taking the remaining wealth, the very wise one went to Kurukshetra, where most of the jewels had been taken.

Having thus brought back his wife, the remnant of the Vrishnis, Dhananjaya settled them here and there, O descendant of Kuru.

The son of Hardikya, the city of Martikavata, and the remaining wives of the king of Bhoja, the best of men,

Then, taking the old, the young, and the women, the son of Pandu  settled all those who were without heroes in Shakra's abode.

He placed Yuyudhana, the beloved son of Sātyaki, in Sarasvati, the righteous one, honored by the old and the young.

The slayer of enemy heroes gave the kingdom to Vajra in Indraprastha. Vajra's sons, being restrained by Akrūra, went forth.

Rukmini, Gandhari, Shaibya, Haimavati, and the goddess Jambavati entered the fire.

Satyabhāma and the other goddesses who were accepted by Krishna, O king, entered the forest, determined to perform austerities.

Those men who lived in Dvārakā and followed Partha, having divided them according to their worth, Jayadratha gave them to the thunderbolt-wielder.

Having done that, Arjuna, his eyes filled with tears, saw Krishna Dvaipāyana seated in the hermitage, O king.
shlokas_raw:
वैशंपायन उवाचएवमुक्तः स बीभत्सुर्मातुलेन परंतपःदुर्मना दीनमनसं वसुदेवमुवाच ह

नाहं वृष्णिप्रवीरेण मधुभिश्चैव मातुलविहीनां पृथिवीं द्रष्टुं शक्तश्चिरमिह प्रभो

राजा च भीमसेनश्च सहदेवश्च पाण्डवःनकुलो याज्ञसेनी च षडेकमनसो वयम्

राज्ञः संक्रमणे चापि कालोऽयं वर्तते ध्रुवम्तमिमं विद्धि संप्राप्तं कालं कालविदां वर

सर्वथा वृष्णिदारांस्तु बालवृद्धांस्तथैव चनयिष्ये परिगृह्याहमिन्द्रप्रस्थमरिंदम

इत्युक्त्वा दारुकमिदं वाक्यमाह धनंजयःअमात्यान्वृष्णिवीराणां द्रष्टुमिच्छामि माचिरम्

इत्येवमुक्त्वा वचनं सुधर्मां यादवीं सभाम्प्रविवेशार्जुनः शूरः शोचमानो महारथान्

तमासनगतं तत्र सर्वाः प्रकृतयस्तथाब्राह्मणा नैगमाश्चैव परिवार्योपतस्थिरे

तान्दीनमनसः सर्वान्निभृतान्गतचेतसःउवाचेदं वचः पार्थः स्वयं दीनतरस्तदा

शक्रप्रस्थमहं नेष्ये वृष्ण्यन्धकजनं स्वयम्इदं तु नगरं सर्वं समुद्रः प्लावयिष्यति

सज्जीकुरुत यानानि रत्नानि विविधानि चवज्रोऽयं भवतां राजा शक्रप्रस्थे भविष्यति

सप्तमे दिवसे चैव रवौ विमल उद्गतेबहिर्वत्स्यामहे सर्वे सज्जीभवत माचिरम्

इत्युक्तास्तेन ते पौराः पार्थेनाक्लिष्टकर्मणासज्जमाशु ततश्चक्रुः स्वसिद्ध्यर्थं समुत्सुकाः

तां रात्रिमवसत्पार्थः केशवस्य निवेशनेमहता शोकमोहेन सहसाभिपरिप्लुतः

श्वोभूतेऽथ ततः शौरिर्वसुदेवः प्रतापवान्युक्त्वात्मानं महातेजा जगाम गतिमुत्तमाम्

ततः शब्दो महानासीद्वसुदेवस्य वेश्मनिदारुणः क्रोशतीनां च रुदतीनां च योषिताम्

प्रकीर्णमूर्धजाः सर्वा विमुक्ताभरणस्रजःउरांसि पाणिभिर्घ्नन्त्यो व्यलपन्करुणं स्त्रियः

तं देवकी च भद्रा च रोहिणी मदिरा तथाअन्वारोढुं व्यवसिता भर्तारं योषितां वराः

ततः शौरिं नृयुक्तेन बहुमाल्येन भारतयानेन महता पार्थो बहिर्निष्क्रामयत्तदा

तमन्वयुस्तत्र तत्र दुःखशोकसमाहताःद्वारकावासिनः पौराः सर्व एव नरर्षभ

तस्याश्वमेधिकं छत्रं दीप्यमानाश्च पावकाःपुरस्तात्तस्य यानस्य याजकाश्च ततो ययुः

अनुजग्मुश्च तं वीरं देव्यस्ता वै स्वलंकृताःस्त्रीसहस्रैः परिवृता वधूभिश्च सहस्रशः

यस्तु देशः प्रियस्तस्य जीवतोऽभून्महात्मनःतत्रैनमुपसंकल्प्य पितृमेधं प्रचक्रिरे

तं चिताग्निगतं वीरं शूरपुत्रं वराङ्गनाःततोऽन्वारुरुहुः पत्न्यश्चतस्रः पतिलोकगाः

तं वै चतसृभिः स्त्रीभिरन्वितं पाण्डुनन्दनःअदाहयच्चन्दनैश्च गन्धैरुच्चावचैरपि

ततः प्रादुरभूच्छब्दः समिद्धस्य विभावसोःसामगानां च निर्घोषो नराणां रुदतामपि

ततो वज्रप्रधानास्ते वृष्णिवीरकुमारकाःसर्व एवोदकं चक्रुः स्त्रियश्चैव महात्मनः

अलुप्तधर्मस्तं धर्मं कारयित्वा स फल्गुनःजगाम वृष्णयो यत्र विनष्टा भरतर्षभ

स तान्दृष्ट्वा निपतितान्कदने भृशदुःखितःबभूवातीव कौरव्यः प्राप्तकालं चकार च

यथाप्रधानतश्चैव चक्रे सर्वाः क्रियास्तदाये हता ब्रह्मशापेन मुसलैरेरकोद्भवैः

ततः शरीरे रामस्य वासुदेवस्य चोभयोःअन्विष्य दाहयामास पुरुषैराप्तकारिभिः

स तेषां विधिवत्कृत्वा प्रेतकार्याणि पाण्डवःसप्तमे दिवसे प्रायाद्रथमारुह्य सत्वरःअश्वयुक्तै रथैश्चापि गोखरोष्ट्रयुतैरपि

स्त्रियस्ता वृष्णिवीराणां रुदत्यः शोककर्शिताःअनुजग्मुर्महात्मानं पाण्डुपुत्रं धनंजयम्

भृत्यास्त्वन्धकवृष्णीनां सादिनो रथिनश्च येवीरहीनं वृद्धबालं पौरजानपदास्तथाययुस्ते परिवार्याथ कलत्रं पार्थशासनात्

कुञ्जरैश्च गजारोहा ययुः शैलनिभैस्तथासपादरक्षैः संयुक्ताः सोत्तरायुधिका ययुः

पुत्राश्चान्धकवृष्णीनां सर्वे पार्थमनुव्रताःब्राह्मणाः क्षत्रिया वैश्याः शूद्राश्चैव महाधनाः

दश षट्च सहस्राणि वासुदेवावरोधनम्पुरस्कृत्य ययुर्वज्रं पौत्रं कृष्णस्य धीमतः

बहूनि च सहस्राणि प्रयुतान्यर्बुदानि चभोजवृष्ण्यन्धकस्त्रीणां हतनाथानि निर्ययुः

तत्सागरसमप्रख्यं वृष्णिचक्रं महर्द्धिमत्उवाह रथिनां श्रेष्ठः पार्थः परपुरंजयः

निर्याते तु जने तस्मिन्सागरो मकरालयःद्वारकां रत्नसंपूर्णां जलेनाप्लावयत्तदा

तदद्भुतमभिप्रेक्ष्य द्वारकावासिनो जनाःतूर्णात्तूर्णतरं जग्मुरहो दैवमिति ब्रुवन्

काननेषु च रम्येषु पर्वतेषु नदीषु चनिवसन्नानयामास वृष्णिदारान्धनंजयः

स पञ्चनदमासाद्य धीमानतिसमृद्धिमत्देशे गोपशुधान्याढ्ये निवासमकरोत्प्रभुः

ततो लोभः समभवद्दस्यूनां निहतेश्वराःदृष्ट्वा स्त्रियो नीयमानाः पार्थेनैकेन भारत

ततस्ते पापकर्माणो लोभोपहतचेतसःआभीरा मन्त्रयामासुः समेत्याशुभदर्शनाः

अयमेकोऽर्जुनो योद्धा वृद्धबालं हतेश्वरम्नयत्यस्मानतिक्रम्य योधाश्चेमे हतौजसः

ततो यष्टिप्रहरणा दस्यवस्ते सहस्रशःअभ्यधावन्त वृष्णीनां तं जनं लोप्त्रहारिणः

महता सिंहनादेन द्रावयन्तः पृथग्जनम्अभिपेतुर्धनार्थं ते कालपर्यायचोदिताः

ततो निवृत्तः कौन्तेयः सहसा सपदानुगःउवाच तान्महाबाहुरर्जुनः प्रहसन्निव

निवर्तध्वमधर्मज्ञा यदि स्थ न मुमूर्षवःनेदानीं शरनिर्भिन्नाः शोचध्वं निहता मया

तथोक्तास्तेन वीरेण कदर्थीकृत्य तद्वचःअभिपेतुर्जनं मूढा वार्यमाणाः पुनः पुनः

ततोऽर्जुनो धनुर्दिव्यं गाण्डीवमजरं महत्आरोपयितुमारेभे यत्नादिव कथंचन

चकार सज्यं कृच्छ्रेण संभ्रमे तुमुले सतिचिन्तयामास चास्त्राणि न च सस्मार तान्यपि

वैकृत्यं तन्महद्दृष्ट्वा भुजवीर्ये तथा युधिदिव्यानां च महास्त्राणां विनाशाद्व्रीडितोऽभवत्

वृष्णियोधाश्च ते सर्वे गजाश्वरथयायिनःन शेकुरावर्तयितुं ह्रियमाणं च तं जनम्

कलत्रस्य बहुत्वात्तु संपतत्सु ततस्ततःप्रयत्नमकरोत्पार्थो जनस्य परिरक्षणे

मिषतां सर्वयोधानां ततस्ताः प्रमदोत्तमाःसमन्ततोऽवकृष्यन्त कामाच्चान्याः प्रवव्रजुः

ततो गाण्डीवनिर्मुक्तैः शरैः पार्थो धनंजयःजघान दस्यून्सोद्वेगो वृष्णिभृत्यैः सह प्रभुः

क्षणेन तस्य ते राजन्क्षयं जग्मुरजिह्मगाःअक्षया हि पुरा भूत्वा क्षीणाः क्षतजभोजनाः

स शरक्षयमासाद्य दुःखशोकसमाहतःधनुष्कोट्या तदा दस्यूनवधीत्पाकशासनिः

प्रेक्षतस्त्वेव पार्थस्य वृष्ण्यन्धकवरस्त्रियःजग्मुरादाय ते म्लेच्छाः समन्ताज्जनमेजय

धनंजयस्तु दैवं तन्मनसाचिन्तयत्प्रभुःदुःखशोकसमाविष्टो निःश्वासपरमोऽभवत्

अस्त्राणां च प्रणाशेन बाहुवीर्यस्य संक्षयात्धनुषश्चाविधेयत्वाच्छराणां संक्षयेण च

बभूव विमनाः पार्थो दैवमित्यनुचिन्तयन्न्यवर्तत ततो राजन्नेदमस्तीति चाब्रवीत्

ततः स शेषमादाय कलत्रस्य महामतिःहृतभूयिष्ठरत्नस्य कुरुक्षेत्रमवातरत्

एवं कलत्रमानीय वृष्णीनां हृतशेषितम्न्यवेशयत कौरव्यस्तत्र तत्र धनंजयः

हार्दिक्यतनयं पार्थो नगरं मार्तिकावतम्भोजराजकलत्रं च हृतशेषं नरोत्तमः

ततो वृद्धांश्च बालांश्च स्त्रियश्चादाय पाण्डवःवीरैर्विहीनान्सर्वांस्ताञ्शक्रप्रस्थे न्यवेशयत्

यौयुधानिं सरस्वत्यां पुत्रं सात्यकिनः प्रियम्न्यवेशयत धर्मात्मा वृद्धबालपुरस्कृतम्

इन्द्रप्रस्थे ददौ राज्यं वज्राय परवीरहावज्रेणाक्रूरदारास्तु वार्यमाणाः प्रवव्रजुः

रुक्मिणी त्वथ गान्धारी शैब्या हैमवतीत्यपिदेवी जाम्बवती चैव विविशुर्जातवेदसम्

सत्यभामा तथैवान्या देव्यः कृष्णस्य संमताःवनं प्रविविशू राजंस्तापस्ये कृतनिश्चयाः

द्वारकावासिनो ये तु पुरुषाः पार्थमन्वयुःयथार्हं संविभज्यैनान्वज्रे पर्यददज्जयः

स तत्कृत्वा प्राप्तकालं बाष्पेणापिहितोऽर्जुनःकृष्णद्वैपायनं राजन्ददर्शासीनमाश्रमे
